rulings.Case Title: DION ROBERTS VS. OCWEN LOAN SERVICING LLC A BUSINESS ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 13, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANTS OCWEN LOAN SERVICING LLC A BUSINESS ENTITY, PHH MORTGAGE CORPORATION, WESTERN PROGRESSIVE, LLC and WELLS FARGO BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS TRUSTEE's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to expressly allege in good faith that the agreement between the beneficiary and servicer (mentioned in Hall letter) does not include a guideline limiting the number of modifications; allege Plaintiff otherwise qualified for a modification; that Plaintiff was ready and willing to pay under the modification and it was the false denial (based on modification guideline limitations) that prevented Plaintiff from becoming current. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NANCY MGGINNIS VS. SELECT PORTFOLIO SERVICING INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Case No. Cgc-21-589693, Case No. Cgc-21-579344, Case No. Cud-21-667620 And/Or Stay Unlawful Detainer Proceedings By Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 13, 2021 line 2. Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Case No. Cgc-21-589693, Case No. Cgc-21-579344, Case No. Cud-21-667620 And/Or Stay Unlawful Detainer Proceedings By Plaintiff is DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. Motion for complete consolidation is DENIED. The actions involve different parties and are not appropriate for complete consolidation. Motion to stay the UD action is granted. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NANCY MGGINNIS VS. SELECT PORTFOLIO SERVICING INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Relief From Violation Of Temporary Restraining Order Or, In The Alternative, December 7 And December 14 Minute Orders Re Real Property Temporary Restraining Order; Memorandum In Support Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 13, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFF NANCY MGGINNIS' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Relief From Violation Of Temporary Restraining Order Or, In The Alternative, December 7 And December 14 Minute Orders Re Real Property Temporary Restraining Order is DENIED. TRO expired by its own terms before December 31, 2020. Unlike in Wutchumna Water Co. v. Superior Court in and for Tulare County (1932) 215 Cal. 734, 736, Plaintiff fails to present any record or other evidence that the Court announced its intention to grant an extension of the TRO/injunction, i.e. "at the conclusion of the hearing the judge merely said: 'the injunction will issue. Bond in the sum of $75,000. Usual conditions of course." The Court also notes that Plaintiff has not requested the return of the bond ordered in conjunction with the TRO, which was requested after years of non-payment on the loan due to the beneficiary's alleged failure to send periodic statements, which payments Plaintiff was ready, willing and able to make. (Second Amended Complaint, paragraphs 11, 12, 30 and 33). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 2400 PACIFIC AVENUE, LLC VS. MATTHEW BENNETT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 13, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANT MATTHEW BENNETT's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action based on nuisance under CCP 1161(4) and SFRO. Complaint is not uncertain. The Court notes that the moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of their filings. Failure to do so in the future will result in motions being taken off calendar. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
